commentary_text: no run fullish delivery pitching around sixth stump, and pushed to short mid-on off the inside half of the bat Previous Next Photos
1 leg bye on a length, and swinging in to pitch on middle. Samson looks to clip, but it hits his pad to dribble to square leg
1 run short of a length around middle and off, and nudged to square leg to get off the mark immediately
no run nicely bowled!! Samson is beaten outside off. Archer bowls full, and around fifth stump. Samson goes driving off the front foot but misses, as the ball zips past the outside edge

FOUR runs edged between short third and the keeper!! The former fielder was brought in just before this ball. Archer goes fullish and just outside off, and Samson drives again. But this time he gets an outside edge right in the gap
1 run a length delivery swinging into middle and off. Clipped to fine leg
1 run shorter side of a length on middle, and angling across from over the wicket. Samson dabs it gently to point for a single
no run fuller side of a length at 140kph. This is floated on leg, and is tapped to cover point

4 leg byes nudged late, and he gets four. In fact, it's given leg byes. Shortish in length, and swinging in to pitch just outside leg. Gaikwad looked to clip, but the ball hit his thigh and beat the keeper
no run extra bounce for Burger. He bowls this on a short of a length leg. Gaikwad looks to nudge, but is hit high on his body
1 run good running. Gaikwad taps this length delivery on middle, and the ball trickles behind the wicket. Gaikwad calls, and Samson responds

OUT bowled him!! The off stump is flattened. Burger fires one at 140kph, and Samson is beaten after looking to heave across the line. On a length on middle, and the ball seams away after pitching. Samson went swinging without any foot movement, and missed. His first innings in yellow, against the side he made a name with, is cut short early Sanju S
no run Archer bowls on a length, and in the corridor just outside off. Gaikwad drives forward, but gets it off the inside half of the to mid-on
no run drives it well, but straight to the man at short cover. Fuller delivery from Archer, and again in the channel outside off, but Gaikwad can't find the gap
no run Another dot. Gaikwad has now faced eight balls for just two runs. Full again, and on off. Pushed to mid-on

no run A frustrated Gaikwad tries something different, but the result remains the same. Short of a length from Archer around off, and Gaikwad slashes by backing away. But the ball goes on the bounce to point

FOUR runs finally he gets one away. The crowd, with plenty of away supporters, is up with that. Another full ball from Archer in that sixth- or seventh-stump channel, and this time Gaikwad drills the ball through cover

OUT gone!! Archer wins the battle against an off-colour Gaikwad. Again the CSK captain looked to make room and attack, but this time failed to get bat on ball. Archer kept the ball fullish and straight, and Gaikwad, looking to open the face of the bat and perhaps slice it, missed. The stumps lit up. There was the swag on Archer's face after launchi

OUT loud appeal for caught behind - and with extreme confidence from Burger and Jurel - but it's turned down. RR review , though. It was a bouncer from Burger that left Mhatre nearly cramped. The ball landed on leg, and took off to pass close to Mhatre's head. He looked to pull that late, but was beaten by the pace and the bounce. Ohh, there's a sp
no run hat-trick is avoided. Burger bowls on the fuller side of a length on off, and Sarfaraz pushes it from deep in the crease to cover
no run ohh, good pace, and a tight line. Short of a length on middle, and Sarfaraz has to push himself back to dab it down off a short of a length

FOUR runs pulled to deep square leg, but not very confidently. Short ball on middle, and seaming away to finish close to off. Sarfaraz doesn't entirely convincingly go behind, but still pulls well enough for four
SIX runs bang!! Sarfaraz countering for CSK. He decides to skip down the pitch, and converts this into a length ball outside leg. He whips it hard over fine leg for the first six of the day

no run RR go up for a caught-behind chance again... and again the umpire is firm. RR don't review this time . Burger bowls on the fuller side, and gets the ball to finish outside off. Sarfaraz went for the drive but was beaten. My first impression was that his bat hit his pad. Nope, the bat hit his pad rather. Replays show the ball would have gone
no run Fuller side of a length around sixth stump, and driven back to Brijesh
no run fullish length around off, and driven to extra cover
no run slapped on the bounce to the man at cover point. Short of a length from Brijesh just outside off, and Short hacks hard at it
no run another dot ball. Brijesh bowls on a short of a length on off, and Short dabs it to cover point
1 run short in length on middle, and Short pulls it to fine leg. The ball didn't get up as much as the length threatened, though

FOUR runs another four for Sarfaraz!! He wasn't entirely in control there, but he'll take it. His top hand came off the bat as he went pulling off this short ball shaping in to finish on leg. The man at short fine leg was beaten comfortably in the end
1 run slower ball first up. Short of a length, and shaping into middle. Clipped to fine leg
1 run fullish ball on middle, and flicked to square leg

OUT chipped to midwicket!! Jaiswal, who took the catch, almost gestures that he couldn't spot the ball. The ball stopped on the pitch before coming on to Short, who ended up pushing at it aerially off a fullish length on the stumps. Or maybe Sandeep with a lack of pace. Either way, Short falls as CSK lose their fourth within the powerplay Matthew S
2 runs Short delivery to the new batter. The ball rises close to middle and off, and Kartik swat-pulls to deep square leg for two quick runs
1 run much fuller ball, and just outside off. This time Kartik leans into a drive to long-on
no run another one at a lack of pace. Sandeep floats this on a fullish length on the stumps, and Sarfaraz blocks it back
no run starts with a quickish ball, which is full and outside off. Driven back by Kartik
1 run short of a length on off, and it straightens after pitching. Punched to cover for a hurried run
1 run short ball at Sarfaraz on off. The latter goes down on his back leg, and pulls to deep midwicket
1 run another short delivery. This turns in from middle, and finishes on leg. Punched to deep midwicket again
1 run short of a length, and slightly wide of off. A late cut to deep point to keep ticking

SIX runs over deep-backward square leg!! Yet another short delivery from Bishnoi, who pitched it on middle. There was hardly any turn either way, and Kartik went back in the crease to dispatch that for his first six
no run on a length, and a little wide of off. Slashed to point while playing away from his body

OUT Jurel and Jadeja are delighted while appealing, and Sarfaraz is given lbw. But he reviews . Sarfaraz went sweeping, but missed, as the ball went beneath his bat. The first replay hints that it could be too high as he's hit on his back, but we'll see. No bat involved is the confirmation from the third umpire. Ohh, in fact, the ball pitches on mi
SIX runs six first ball!! CSK's designated spin hitter says I'll do it right away. A free swing of the arms off this full ball around off, and the ball disappears over long-on
no run looks to jab off this length ball outside off, but is beaten
no run outside edge, but dribbling to short third. This ball is quicker and shorter, and outside off

OUT number six!! Jadeja's experience trumps Dube's aggression. The RR spinner goes full and wider of off, tempting Dube to launch him down the ground again. Dube takes the bait, but ends up finding long-off. It was a bit of a cat and mouse between the two right from the first ball, and Jadeja won't mind taking the wicket after being hit for six fir
2 runs very full, and outside off. Kartik leans into the drive wide of long-off
no run short in length, and turning in to finish close to off. Cut away to point
1 run fuller and flatter, and fired at 103kph. Driven from outside off to cover point
no run good bounce off a length around sixth stump. He goes back to block to Bishnoi
1 run very full, and outside off again. Dragged to midwicket
1 run short in length, and again at 103kph. This arrives around sixth stump, and is slapped to deep-backward point
no run On a length on off, and slightly turning away to beat Kartik, who looked to defend
no run fuller ball, and straighter. Driven back to Jadeja

1 run this could have been tight, but the throw was too high for Jurel to collect. Jaiswal at point rushed forward and threw, but too high. Kartik defended this full ball outside off towards point, and took off
no run full and quick, and darted into off. Overton pushes it back
SIX runs pinged down the ground!! He simply leans into the loft off this fullish ball on off, as he skips down the ground to attack. A rare positive for CSK
1 run fullish ball on middle, and nudged to midwicket as Jadeja himself gives the chase
1 run Action seems similar to Sreesanth's. Shortish ball on leg stump, not much bounce, inside edge off the flick goes off the body to the off side
2 runs Nudged smartly to the edge of the ring at midwicket and they steal two. Length ball on middle and leg

OUT That should be out, finger goes up. Kartik chats... and takes the review. 134ks full ball on middle and leg. Kartik's forward stride was small and he tried to drive it through mid-on. Bat well in front of body, beat the inside-edge and hit the outer-half of the front pad. On the shin. Ball-tracking shows it's hitting leg stump and Brijesh has h
no run Shortish ball rises around the ribs, 132ks, worked back to the bowler
no run Edge? No. Noor goes for the pull, the bouncer is around middle stump and just over the shoulder. Goes above the top edge
no run Slower shortish ball just outside off, defended to cover point. 105ks
1 run Full outside off, driven to long-off
1 run Lunges forward and knocks the length ball on off to long-off
no run Length just down leg, Overton slides leg side and looks to flick. Misses and gets hit on the pad

2 runs They steal two again, inside edge works in Overton's favour as the ball goes in the vacant midwicket region. Fullish ball down leg, Overton was sliding down leg too, looked to defend straight with bat a bit away from the body
1 leg bye Touch short, too straight. Overton looks to pul it fine, misses and gets pad on it. Goes to short fine leg
no run Slowed down and that buys turn. Lands on a length just outside off and turns away sharply. Noor looks to punch through cover, gets a thick edge to short third
3 runs Driven through extra with bottom hand gently sliding off the bat. Mid-off has to chase, does well Parag, slides and palms the ball in close to the boundary. Archer just a touch too full
no run Pitched up on off, driven to mid-off
no run Pacy ball halfway down. Lands around off and goes away outside off, Noor looks to pull but it's too far away
no run Another pull beaten, sharper bouncer this time. Over the stumps, Noor was beside the line of the ball and swung well under it

OUT Thick edge, keeper takes it. Noor shows all his stumps, hoping to get one maybe around middle or off. It goes just outside off and he looks to slash it through cover. He's a bit too far away again and hands a regulation catch Noor Ahmad c †Jurel b Archer 1 (9b 0x4 0x6 9m) SR: 11.11 Previous Next Photos
no run No, but Henry has a go. Misses. Full ball outside off, not dissimilar from the last one. Henry stands still and looks to slap it over cover
2 runs Overton wants the strike, it'll be a tight second... He makes it. Length on leg, flicked to midwicket
no run Backs away and the length ball from around the wicket arrows into him. Punches it back to the bowler
no run Swipes and misses. Back of length just outside off, goes with a flat-bat, bottom hand almost comes off and clanks inward
no run Hits the back bicep, on the fleshy part close to the elbow. Back of length ball on off rushes him as he tries to flick it square. Ball goes to the off side. Physio comes to check in
no run Length on leg, defended to cover
1 run Short just outside off, tennis-style swat-pull goes to long-on
1 run Touch fuller than length just outside off, square driven to deep point
no run Too wide for the pull. Pre-meditated shot, Henry hopped and tried to drag it leg side from around head-height. Misses. Good bounce on that
no run Yorker attempted, full toss delivered. Henry may have though that was higher than it was, takes an extra second and flicks it to midwicket
FOUR runs Edge goes through first slip. Touch too high and wide for the keeper. Henry backing away as he pulls this back of length ball around hip-height
1 run Slower one outside off, length ball is dragged to long-on
1 run Slower bouncer has been picked up... Jaiswal does nicely at deep midwicket. Dives left and saves three. Overton hit that into the ground from around head-height. Timeout
1 run Flat and fast on middle, looks to defend straight. Inside edge goes to square leg
no run Back of length on leg, 94ks which is a touch slower for him, beats the flick and hits pad

OUT Lobbed back to the bowler. Bishnoi takes a low catch, falls back, gets up and raises his arms. Slowed down again, it's full and on off. Henry lunged forward and was too early into the drive. Didn't check his shot and has to walk back Matt Henry c & b Ravi Bishnoi 5 (7b 1x4 0x6 16m) SR: 71.42
no run Length outside off, punched to cover
no run Length down leg, goes past the leg glance. Keeper half-stops it
no run Full and a touch slow again, looks to flick to midwicket. Hits pad and goes into the ground
no run Into the pitch just outside off, looks to pull it across and misses. Ball goes over bat
no run Beaten again. This was rapid. Back of length, trying to follow the batter as he made room. Beaten on the pull
no run Full toss goes over the stumps, surprising how it didn't hit. Overton went across early for the lap, tried to scoop it and missed. Ball went over middle
no run Beaten again, two more balls left Jofra says to Overton as he grunts. He checks with the square-leg ump for height. Not given. Was a short ball on leg, he tried to pull it
FOUR runs Thick edge flies and rolls to the deep-third fence, Archer smiles. Cleared the front leg and tried to drive the full ball over cover.
1 run Yorker nailed on off, jammed to long-off
FOUR runs Slower bouncer has been swat-pulled from head-high. Top edge flies over short fine leg and rolls into the cushion
1 run Full and slow outside off, whipped into the outfield. Rolls to long-on
1 leg bye Too straight, Kamboj goes to off stump and inside the line of the length ball. Looks to flick fine, misses, ball goes fine off the pad anyway
1 run Chipped... just past the bowler. Sandeep jumped to the right in follow-through as the ball floated but it was too far. Pitched-up legcutter foxed Overton, too early into the straight drive
1 run Length on off, flicked to deep midwicket
1 run Length on leg, flicked to deep square leg
1 run Length over off, clears the front leg and slaps it to cover

FOUR runs Free hit is a slower one that's helped away with one hand. Bottom hand comes off as Kamboj went for the pull, was too early into the shot but got enough for it to go over backward square leg
1 leg bye Looks to slice it across the line, the length ball beats him and hits the thigh. Deflects to the off side
1 run Wide on a length, caressed with a flat-bat to long-off
1 run Edge goes through first slip but there's essentially a deep first slip too. Stopped there. Length ball just outside off, Kamboj tried to punch it through cover
1 leg bye Bouncer seems to have hit him on the grille as Overton swivelled for the pull. It was on the peak of the helmet replay shows. Goes to short fine leg. Concussion test will take place
SIX runs Met with! No venom on the bouncer, landed very short but didn't rise much. Overton made room and pulled it, almost got a whiplash as the ball sailed over wide long-on
1 run Shows the stumps, drives the yorker on leg to long-off. Can't nick a two
1 run Kamboj manages to turn strike. Length ball on leg, flicked to midwicket and the 'CSK' chants return

1 run, OUT Has a hoick, doesn't time it. Rolls to deep midwicket, Hetmyer throws it casually to the keeper. Overton ambled the first, as he finished the run he realised he can nick a second. But the throw, on the bounce, came to the keeper at a good height. Off went the bails and he ran through towards the pavillion Jamie Overton run out (Hetmyer/†
